title: The Substance
logline: A fading celebrity fitness instructor uses a black-market serum to create a younger, better version of herself, but the two bodies must share time in a strict seven-day cycle—a balance that becomes increasingly difficult to maintain as the younger self refuses to give up her newfound fame.
protagonist: Elisabeth Sparkle / Sue (two bodies, one consciousness)
antagonistOrConflict: The imbalance between Elisabeth and Sue; the impossible demands of youth and beauty; Harvey and the entertainment industry
tone: Visceral, satirical, increasingly nightmarish, with moments of dark absurdism
themes: The impossibility of balance when pursuing perfection

actBreaks.description: Sue emerges from the bathroom for the first time as the activated 'other self,' marking the full commitment to the Substance and the beginning of the dual existence.

validation.ruling: Scene #31 (p31, bathroom) marks the irreversible transformation where NewElisabeth emerges, discovers her new voice, experiences the first nosebleed, and must inject Stabilizer into the matrix body—fundamentally changing the story from 'will she take The Substance?' to 'how will she manage two selves?' This is the last scene before the new reality begins.

actBreaks.description: Harvey fires Sue from the New Year's Eve show, destroying the illusion that Sue can maintain her success indefinitely and forcing a crisis in the balance.

validation.ruling: Scene #170 (p96, Harvey's office) is the act break where Harvey offers Sue the New Year's Eve special, forcing her into higher stakes and accelerating her addiction to The Substance. This major turning point at 65.8% propels the story into its final act of escalating desperation and body horror as Sue pursues ultimate fame.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This script operates as a tightly wound descent into body horror that mirrors its protagonist's psychological unraveling. The first act establishes Elisabeth's world and humiliation with clinical precision—her firing happens by scene 7, the Substance is introduced by scene 15, and she's activated Sue by scene 31. The structure is relentlessly forward-moving, with almost no subplots or digressions. 

The second act (scenes 32-170) is where Fargeat's structural audacity becomes clear: she uses repetition and ritual to show the gradual erosion of the seven-day balance. Scenes of switching, injecting, and waking become a hypnotic pattern that slowly degrades. The script doesn't rely on traditional mid-act complications but instead on accumulating transgressions—Sue taking an extra day here, Elisabeth's body deteriorating there. The midpoint isn't a single revelation but a creeping realization that the balance cannot hold. By scene 170, when Harvey fires Sue, the structure has already primed us for catastrophe through sheer repetitive dread.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deceptive: scenes are extremely short (1.2 pages average), creating a staccato rhythm that accelerates the sensation of time slipping away. The first 30 scenes cover Elisabeth's entire fall and decision to use the Substance in roughly 30 pages—breakneck for a character study. The middle section slows into ritualistic repetition (scenes 40-100 are dominated by switching sequences, injections, and body maintenance), which paradoxically creates mounting tension through monotony. The final act (scenes 171-246) explodes into 75 scenes of escalating grotesquerie, with the New Year's Eve sequence (scenes 218-241) functioning as an extended nightmare climax that refuses to resolve cleanly.

incitingIncident.description: Harvey eats shrimp while delivering a monologue about renewal being inevitable and people always asking for something new, as Elisabeth sits across from him at a restaurant.

validation.ruling: Scene #7 (hospital room) is Elisabeth's emotional reaction to being fired, not the inciting incident itself. Scene #4 (p9, restaurant) is the true inciting incident where Harvey fires Elisabeth from her show, shattering her status quo and establishing the central conflict that drives the entire narrative—her desperation to reclaim her career and youth.

midpoint.description: Elisabeth sits at the kitchen table and calls the phone number, identifying herself as Elisabeth Sparkle from Beverly Drive, number 503; after an embarrassed pause while staring at her deformed finger, she tells the cavernous voice on the other end that she wants to stop.

validation.ruling: Scene #121 (p67, kitchen phone call) is a textbook midpoint where Elisabeth learns 'there is no going back' after misusing The Substance, shifting her from reactive victim to someone who must now live with irreversible consequences. This point of no return at 45.9% raises stakes significantly and marks her transformation from hoping to fix things to accepting permanent damage.

climax.description: The live countdown begins as a backlit figure walks onto the soundstage and takes her place among the dancers, revealing MonstroElisaSue in the lights as complete silence falls, then Harvey screams for her to get off his stage and she attacks him, biting his face and tearing his head off before the crowd attacks her and she explodes blood everywhere.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. NEW YEAR EVE STAGE - NIGHT

validation.ruling: Scene #241 (p141, New Year's Eve stage) is the climax where MonstroElisaSue appears live on stage before Harvey, the shareholders, dancers, and cameras—the ultimate confrontation that resolves the main story question of whether she can reclaim her place in the spotlight. This is peak dramatic tension at 96.6%, demonstrating the horrific completion of her character arc.

scenes.heading: EXT. WALK OF FAME SIDEWALK - DAY
scenes.summary: Workers construct Elisabeth Sparkle's star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, placing brass letters spelling her name and sealing a pink marble slab into the terrazzo pavement.

scenes.heading: INT. TV STUDIO / SPARKLE YOUR LIFE SHOW - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth Sparkle, nearly fifty, leads an aerobics class on her TV show 'Sparkle Your Life' with forced smiles and ultra-sculpted body under bright studio lights.

scenes.heading: INT. BACKSTAGE HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks down the backstage corridor receiving birthday wishes, passes posters showing her aging over the years, and enters the men's room after finding the ladies' room closed.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: Harvey eats shrimp while delivering a monologue about renewal being inevitable and people always asking for something new, as Elisabeth sits across from him at a restaurant.

scenes.heading: INT. CRAIG SILVER MANAGEMENT OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Craig Silver informs Elisabeth that Toothbrite is ending her ambassador contract due to significant change in her public notoriety with the end of her show.

scenes.heading: EXT. AVENUE - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth drives past a billboard with her Toothbrite ad when another car smashes into hers at a crossroad, causing the windows to explode and the car to spin violently.
scenes.turningPointNote: Inciting incident — the car accident that leads Elisabeth to the hospital where she receives the USB stick

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: A doctor tells Elisabeth she has no injuries from the accident and can leave, while a stunningly handsome young male nurse with piercing blue eyes hands the doctor her file.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL. - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks out of the hospital, finds a USB stick labeled 'The Substance' with a note saying 'It changed my life' in her coat pocket, and is recognized by an unprepossessing-looking man.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elisabeth discovers the USB stick that will introduce her to The Substance

scenes.heading: INT. ELISABETH’S APARTMENT / LIVING ROOM - END OF AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Elisabeth plugs the USB stick into her TV and watches a completely black screen while a deep male voice asks if she's ever dreamt of a better version of herself.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKTAIL LOUNGE BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth sits at a cocktail lounge bar and empties three martini glasses in succession.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT / LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the dark apartment, someone throws up while wilted roses sit asphyxiated in their plastic wrapping on the table.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth kneels over the toilet, then stands at the sink splashing water on her face, making her mascara run down her cheeks like a sad clown.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth stands at the picture window looking at the city lights, picks up a snow globe with a figurine of herself on a pink star, and contemplates her awards and photos.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth throws something in the trash, then opens the can again and retrieves the USB stick covered in sticky residue.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth calls a number from her bed at night, and a deep masculine voice asks for her address after she says she'd like to order.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elisabeth commits to ordering The Substance

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM / CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth wakes up with a hangover still dressed on her bed, gets up, and greets her cleaning lady Maria in the hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOWER. DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth stands under a powerful jet of water in the shower as it washes away the traces of makeup on her face.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN / LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth drops an effervescent aspirin tablet into water and sits at the kitchen table in a bathrobe while Maria vacuums in the background.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (ELISABETH AREA). DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks down the street in her mustard yellow coat, followed by a hand-held camera from behind.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (LESS NICE STREET). DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth continues walking through a less pleasant area of the street.

scenes.heading: EXT. AVENUE / DEPOSIT BUILDING (SKID ROW). DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth arrives at a seedy metal shutter door, waves her card to open it, and crouches under the half-stuck shutter to enter.

scenes.heading: INT. DEPOSIT / LONG NARROW CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks down a long narrow decrepit hallway with a pest control notification visible.

scenes.heading: INT. DEPOSIT / LOBBY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth enters a small windowless lobby with letter boxes, finds boxes marked 207 and 503, and waves her card to unlock her box.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT / LIVING ROOM - END OF THE DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth opens a cardboard box marked 503 and lays out its contents: an Activator vial, Stabilizer kit, surgical supplies, and instruction cards.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth stands naked before her bathroom mirror examining her aging body, fills a syringe with fluorescent yellow Activator liquid, makes a tourniquet on her arm, and injects herself.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elisabeth injects The Substance, triggering her transformation

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A new version of Elisabeth sits at the kitchen table in a bathrobe making guttural sounds to test her voice, spitting fluorescent yellow mucus before speaking clearly.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The new Elisabeth lies in bed staring at the ceiling, turns over repeatedly unable to sleep, then gets out of bed and turns on the light.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The new Elisabeth returns to the bathroom, kneels beside the matrix body on the floor, tilts it onto its back, places a folded towel under its head, and checks its breathing.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. BEDROOM WINDOW / PALM TREE - DAY
scenes.summary: A shiny-leaved palm tree stands out against the blue sky outside the bedroom window.

scenes.heading: INT. TOP SHOT SHOWER. DAY
scenes.summary: The new Elisabeth stands under the shower jet, running her hands along her waist, belly, and thighs to appreciate her new body.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM. DAY
scenes.summary: The new Elisabeth walks past the matrix body on the bathroom floor, dries her hair, experiences a nosebleed, and takes out the Stabilizer kit to inject herself in the thigh.

scenes.heading: INT. FRIDGE - DAY
scenes.summary: The new Elisabeth puts the tube containing six remaining doses of Stabilizer into the fridge.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The new Elisabeth stands facing the picture window, looks at the framed photo of Elisabeth on the wall, then performs a full split with disconcerting ease.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (ELISABETH AREA) - DAY
scenes.summary: The new Elisabeth walks down the street in Elisabeth's oversized mustard coat with an unsteady step, aware of heightened sensory stimuli.

scenes.heading: I/E. SHOP WINDOW / STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The new Elisabeth stands in front of a shop window looking at a plastic mannequin's perfect body in a flashy pink leotard before entering the shop.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (SHOP EXIT) - DAY
scenes.summary: The new Elisabeth walks down the street in a pink varsity jacket, pleated miniskirt, and white sneakers with an assured swaying gait as heads turn to watch her.

scenes.heading: EXT. STUDIO LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of a studio lot.

scenes.heading: INT. DANCE STUDIO / CASTING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: A casting director dismisses a girl, makes a crude comment about her nose, then looks up as the new Elisabeth enters and their faces freeze in awe.

scenes.heading: INT. HARVEY’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Harvey charges into his office where Sue stands surrounded by screens showing close-ups of her lips, makes her spin like a figurine, sits her down, and offers her Elisabeth's show.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sue secures Elisabeth's former show, completing the replacement

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (FULL GLAM & POP) - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue walks down the street with full confidence and an assured swaying gait as heads turn outright to watch her pass.

scenes.heading: INT. FRIDGE/KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue opens the fridge and grabs the puncture syringe and vials.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM
scenes.summary: Sue unclips a new vial from the syringe barrel, gives herself an intramuscular injection in the thigh, and the empty vial falls into the trash with four others.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Sue signs Elisabeth's name on a note with cash for Maria explaining an unexpected trip abroad, and puts it in an envelope.

scenes.heading: INT. WALK-IN CLOSET - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue pushes Elisabeth's entire wardrobe to either side of the walk-in closet in one sharp gesture and hangs up her new brightly colored clothing.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM. DAY
scenes.summary: Sue unwraps a new pink phone, sets a ringtone, and places it next to Elisabeth's gold phone.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT CORRIDOR / LANDING. DAY
scenes.summary: Through the peephole, Sue watches Maria read the note and look at the cash in the envelope.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM. DAY
scenes.summary: Sue replaces the white cotton bedsheet with powder pink satin and lets herself fall back onto it as an IV bag slowly empties above her.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue stands in her silk dressing gown with an embroidered dragon facing the picture window at night as a slight tinnitus buzzes in her ears.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Sue looks at herself in the mirror as the last drops drain from the matrix's IV bag and the seven vials are used, then she unclips the empty bag, attaches the switch pipe, and sticks the needle into the matrix's arm.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sue performs the first switch back to Elisabeth

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth wakes up with a start in her old body on the bathroom floor, coughing uncontrollably as Sue's lifeless body collapses on top of her, then she rolls Sue off and staggers up weakly.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth cooks and eats eggs ravenously in the kitchen while Sue's body lies motionless on the bathroom floor in the darkness.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The living room sits still in sunlight with Elisabeth's poster visible on the wall.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOWER - DAY
scenes.summary: Water streams down over the large stitches along Elisabeth's spine in the shower.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth receives a note informing her that her two-week refill kit has been delivered to her deposit box.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth removes the sutures from her healed back scar with surgical pliers, then answers a ringing phone.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Harvey calls Elisabeth asking her to come empty her office, then hangs up abruptly, leaving her sitting on the bed with her scar visible.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (ELISABETH AREA) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks down the street in her mustard coat, repeatedly adjusting her collar to hide her scar as no one notices her.

scenes.heading: EXT. STUDIO LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of the studio lot.

scenes.heading: INT. STUDIO / BACKSTAGE CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth stares at the backstage corridor where all her photos have been removed, then Harvey appears and hands her a box of her belongings.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (STUDIO AREA) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks with a stressed step on the street near the studio.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (LESS NICE STREET) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks through a less pleasant area of the street.

scenes.heading: INT. DEPOSIT CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks down the narrow deposit corridor.

scenes.heading: INT. DEPOSIT LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth waves her card over deposit box 503.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth steps over Sue's motionless body, retrieves the new supply kit from the box, stores it in the cabinet, and sees four more days remaining on the IV bag.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN/LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth crosses out days on a calendar, writing 'SUE' in alternating seven-day blocks for the entire month, then sits bored listening to the radio.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth opens the bathroom door to check on Sue lying on the floor with two days remaining on the IV, then closes the door again as time passes.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN / LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue stores the compartmentalized syringe in the fridge, grabs a soda, and moves through the apartment with music blasting.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue looks down at Elisabeth's slumped body on the bathroom floor, crushes her soda can, and begins tapping on walls searching for something.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue taps on walls inside the bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. WALK-IN CLOSET - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue opens the walk-in closet, pushes clothes aside, and discovers the wall sounds hollow.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue taps the bathroom wall and discovers a hollow space between the closet and bathroom, then stares at the wall pensively.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM IN CONSTRUCTION - DAY
scenes.summary: A sledgehammer blow strikes in darkness, shaking the frame and releasing dust.

scenes.heading: INT. CASTING ROOM / DANCE STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Dancers land in splits on a dance studio floor during rehearsal.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM IN CONSTRUCTION - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue breaks through the wall with a sledgehammer, creating a hole that lets light through.

scenes.heading: INT. CASTING ROOM / DANCE STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue leads dancers in rhythmic clapping during a dance studio rehearsal.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM / BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue widens the hole in the bathroom wall with repeated sledgehammer blows, revealing a dark cavity and creating clouds of dust.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth's matrix lies motionless in the living room under her framed poster while sledgehammer blows sound off-screen.

scenes.heading: INT. PHOTO STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue poses for a photo shoot in a studio as the camera flashes.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: A blowtorch welds elements inside the wall cavity.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue packs Elisabeth's awards and photos into a cardboard box and closes it, then a sewing machine runs at full speed.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue drills wall plugs into the wall until someone bangs loudly on the front door.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM / HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue opens the door to the neighbor who came to complain about noise, tells him Elisabeth moved out and introduces herself as the new tenant, then closes the door on him.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - JOUR
scenes.summary: Sue walks back into the living room where a huge billboard of her in a pink leotard is visible outside, then drags Elisabeth's matrix down the hallway with the IV bag propped on her belly.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sue has fully taken over Elisabeth's life and space, literally erasing her presence and relegating her body to hidden storage.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue drags Elisabeth's matrix down the corridor with noticeably less care, letting her hip knock furniture and her arm catch on objects.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue reaches the bathroom and opens the door to the secret room.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue drags Elisabeth's matrix into the dark secret room built behind the bathroom wall, which has an invisible sliding wall and only a small air vent for circulation.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue's picture replaces Elisabeth's in the living room as Elisabeth's large framed poster is removed from the wall.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue stores Elisabeth's framed poster in the secret room, stares at the matrix, then closes the heavy wall, plunging Elisabeth into total darkness.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elisabeth is sealed away in complete darkness and isolation, marking a major shift in the power dynamic and Sue's treatment of her matrix.

scenes.heading: INT. TV STUDIO / SUE’S SHOW “PUMP IT UP” - DAY
scenes.summary: A spotlight reveals Sue and dancers in colorful leotards on the set of her new show 'Pump It Up' as she introduces the program and begins an energetic aerobics routine.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sue's new show launches, completing her takeover of Elisabeth's career and public identity.

scenes.heading: INT. HARVEY’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: The show plays on the wall of screens in Harvey's office.

scenes.heading: INT. TV STUDIO / SUE’S SHOW “PUMP IT UP” - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue finishes the routine with the dancers in perfect sync, breathless and sweating, then signs off telling viewers to take care of themselves.

scenes.heading: INT. TV STUDIO BACKSTAGE CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue walks through the backstage corridor with glistening eyes, stops to drink water while passing a poster advertising her new show.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. TV STUDIO EXIT DOOR / STUDIO LOT ALLEY - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue exits the building into sunlight when Craig calls out to her, introducing himself and expressing his desire to work with her.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM /HALLWAY /BATHROOM /SECRET ROOM - END OF DAY
scenes.summary: Sue opens a box containing a leather catsuit and slides it on in extreme close-up as the material becomes one with her body.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM / SECRET ROOM - END OF DAY
scenes.summary: Sue applies bright red lipstick in the mirror as her eyes glow and horns honk outside.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Sue crouches next to Elisabeth's matrix in the secret room, tells her not to eat too fast and that she won't be back late, then closes the door leaving Elisabeth in darkness.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (ELISABETH BUILDING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue gets into a convertible filled with dancers who welcome her with whistles and laughter as the car speeds off into the night.

scenes.heading: INT. APT / LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The silent living room sits empty with the outline of the removed picture frame visible on the wall as time passes.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth's body lies motionless in the secret room with glazed eyes staring into nothing as the nutrition bag reaches nearly empty and a muffled echo sounds in the distance.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue stumbles up the stairwell drunk with a man, stopping repeatedly to kiss passionately and laugh.

scenes.heading: INT. LANDING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A man pushes Sue against her apartment door and kisses her aggressively, grabbing her body through her leather catsuit before she gently pulls away and says she has to go.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The last drops drain from Elisabeth's IV bag in the secret room as sounds of Sue arriving home with someone are heard—footsteps, laughter, then the crash of breaking glass.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue and the man, both drunk, laugh about the broken glass, fall onto the sofa, and begin kissing as a tinnitus buzzes in Sue's ears.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The empty perfusion bag begins to shrivel and make squeaking noises as it tries to suck up fluid that isn't there.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue and the man kiss more intensely as a drop of blood falls on his white t-shirt, which Sue ignores while removing his shirt and sliding her hands down his chest.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The empty perfusion bag crumples with increasingly high-pitched plastic sounds.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue reaches for the man's trousers and unbuckles his belt.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The perfusion bag desperately tries to suck something in with intensifying sounds.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue unbuttons the man's trousers as blood drops fall rapidly on his chest; the sound becomes muffled and the image flickers as she feels overwhelmed, hears her accelerating heartbeat, and staggers away saying she needs a minute.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM / SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue locks herself in the bathroom and discovers the empty nutrition bag as Elisabeth's pale body mechanically tries to suck up what isn't there; blood drops from Sue's nose increase as tinnitus blares and pain becomes unbearable, forcing her to switch.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sue realizes she has stayed too long and must switch back to Elisabeth despite being in the middle of a sexual encounter—the first clear consequence of violating the balance.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue clips an empty vial onto the puncture syringe, lifts the bandage on her back, hesitates briefly while looking at the puncture site, then slowly pushes the long needle into Elisabeth's spinal column and pulls the plunger.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue reappears straddling the man and begins kissing him greedily as he places his hands on her thighs and moves them toward her body.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth's limp body lies still on the cold tiles in the dark room with her foot dangling as the second food bag slowly empties into her arm.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The man's hands work their way up Sue's body from her thighs to her waist.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Yellowish paste makes its way up the IV tube with a gurgling noise in the dark room.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The man's hands slide up Sue's back to her neck and pull down the zipper of her catsuit, which appears to open Sue's body as her internal organs spew out and splatter on the floor in a bloody mass.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth wakes up screaming in the secret room, coughing and clutching her throat as if asphyxiated; she realizes the nightmare has ended but discovers an empty vial on the ground and the second perfusion bag already half-emptied, then clips the vial back onto the syringe.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elisabeth wakes to discover Sue has stolen extra stabilizer fluid—the first concrete evidence of Sue's violation and the beginning of physical consequences for Elisabeth.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth puts on her bathrobe and looks dreadful in the mirror with an enormous hangover and a sore back.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR / LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks down the hallway and discovers Sue's clothing strewn on the floor leading to an unmade bed with rumpled sheets; she tries to remember the night before but cannot recall anything, then finds the living room filled with aftermath of festivities including half-empty glasses, full ashtrays, and a broken glass.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth hurries to the bathroom sink and scrubs her index finger under cold water, but the deformation remains—horribly withered skin, protruding swollen blue veins, and deformed knuckles; she closes her eyes as understanding hardens her expression, then looks at Sue's body and the empty vial.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elisabeth discovers her finger has aged and deformed—the first visible, permanent physical consequence of Sue taking more than her share, marking the beginning of Elisabeth's bodily deterioration.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth sits at the kitchen table and calls the phone number, identifying herself as Elisabeth Sparkle from Beverly Drive, number 503; after an embarrassed pause while staring at her deformed finger, she tells the cavernous voice on the other end that she wants to stop.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elisabeth decides to stop using the Substance and calls to terminate—a major decision that could end the entire arrangement with Sue.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Elisabeth brushes her teeth at night, staring at her gnarled finger on the toothbrush; she completes her nighttime routine with cream, then hesitates before putting the jar away.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth lies perfectly still in bed staring at the ceiling with her bandaged hand resting on the sheets, dominated by the palm tree's shadow that looks like a giant spider.

scenes.heading: I/E. BEDROOM / WINDOW PALM TREE - DAY
scenes.summary: Morning sunlight reveals the outline where Elisabeth lay perfectly still all night in bed.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth unwraps the bandage from her finger, dissolves the dried cream crust under water, wipes her hand, and discovers nothing has changed—her finger is still wrinkled and deformed by arthritis.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: A new note card on the kitchen table announces the two-week refill kit has been delivered; in the background, Elisabeth pushes a vacuum cleaner back and forth across the living room.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth vacuums the carpet intensely as if hunting for dust, then looks up and stops when something catches her attention; she turns off the vacuum and discovers Sue energetically performing on the television screen in her new show, causing Elisabeth a moment of befuddled confusion.

scenes.heading: INT. APT ENTRANCE / APT CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: The neighbor Oliver buzzes Elisabeth's door repeatedly, calls for Sue, mentions seeing her on TV, asks about private lessons and a drink at his place, then leaves after a long silence.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOWER - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth stands in the shower with her hand against the wall for support, letting water pummel her back as she stares at the ground lost in thought.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT / ENTRANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth puts on her coat, turns up the collar, puts on leather gloves to hide her problem finger, and checks through the peephole that the coast is clear.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (ELISABETH AREA) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks quickly down the street.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (LESS NICE STREET)- DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks down another, less nice street with a jittery gait as everything seems more harsh and aggressive.

scenes.heading: INT. DEPOSIT CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks down the deposit corridor.

scenes.heading: INT. DEPOSIT/LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth waves her card to open her locker, hears a slight noise behind her and turns to scan the empty lobby, then takes out the package and puts it in her bag.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (LESS NICE STREET) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks in the opposite direction, feels like she's being followed, turns around to see no one, then picks up the pace.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREE (DINER AREA) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth continues walking, turns around again seeing nothing, then goes into a diner.

scenes.heading: INT. DINER - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth sits in a booth, moves to another, removes then readjusts her glove when a waitress startles her; she orders a mocha latte, then a sweaty obese man with a strawberry birthmark at the next table tells her seven days is long and reveals he also uses the Substance, warning her to respect the balance or she'll end up like him.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (DINRE AREA) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth returns home with a hurried step.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (ELISABETH BUILDING) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth looks over her shoulder several times then violently slams into someone—a biker whose helmet falls and rolls away; she discovers it's Troy, who angrily tells her to watch out and storms off on his bike.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT / ENTRANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth slams her door shut and leans against it for a long while, trying to channel the thoughts and feelings racing obsessively in her brain.

scenes.heading: INT. WALK IN CLOSET - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth pushes aside Sue's clothing in the closet, opens boxes marked as her old junk, shakes out handbags until she finds the torn crinkled paper with Fred's number on it.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth starts dialing Fred's number while her eyes glance inadvertently at Sue's billboard outside the window.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth sits in the bathroom and calls Fred, who is initially silent when she identifies herself as Lizzie from tenth grade homeroom; he then expresses shock and excitement, mentions her show cancellation, and asks her out for dinner, which she accepts for Friday at eight.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Elisabeth tries on a beautiful moiré silk dress that hides her back scar, adds long black satin gloves to hide her problem finger, and admires the glamorous effect in the mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - END OF THE DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth finishes her makeup nervously as the date approaches, flashes a satisfied smile in the mirror, then goes to close the secret room door and catches sight of Sue's healthy complexion and plump lips, making her suddenly feel her entire face sag.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY / LIVING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks to get her keys and looks up at Sue's huge billboard outside showing her perfectly round breasts, luscious lips, and gorgeous hair.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Elisabeth returns to the bathroom mirror with a harsh expression, pulls up her dress fabric to hide her cleavage, throws a wrap over her shoulders, changes her lipstick, adds more blush in increasingly aggressive brush strokes, lets her hair down then puts it back up, and forces a smile.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR / ENTRANCE / LIVING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Elisabeth walks down the hallway forcing herself not to look in the living room, but when about to open the front door she sees her distorted reflection in the metal doorknob and senses Sue's billboard looming behind her.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Elisabeth stands in front of the bathroom mirror with a dour, accusatory look, thinking "disgusting," then wipes the lipstick off her face with a slow harsh gesture that smears it onto her cheek like a bloody gash.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth sits on the bed in her bathrobe with makeup removed and skin reddened from repeatedly applying and removing it, slightly stooped facing the window and the crushing verticality of the huge palm tree, as her phone lights up with multiple unanswered messages from Fred.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elisabeth cannot bring herself to go on the date with Fred, paralyzed by self-loathing and comparison to Sue—a complete psychological collapse that marks her inability to exist as herself anymore.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth sits motionless in the dark living room with the TV on while a neighbor knocks at the door calling for Sue, then eventually gets up and shuffles across the room.

scenes.heading: INT. FRIDGE / KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The bathrobed figure stands motionless in front of the open refrigerator, then grabs leftover quiche and closes the door.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR / BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: A figure showers in the steam-filled bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOWER - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue enjoys the hot shower water on her body, then discovers a small bulge under the skin of her buttock surrounded by cellulite that disappears when she releases pressure.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue feels something on her butt while sitting on the bed, checks herself in the mirror extensively, but sees nothing wrong with her backside.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (GLAM & POP) - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue walks down the street with a lively step in hot, colorful surroundings.

scenes.heading: INT. TV STUDIO / SUE’S DRESSING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue changes into her leotard in the dressing room and checks her ass in the mirror, finding it impeccable.

scenes.heading: INT. BACKSTAGE HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue walks onto the soundstage.

scenes.heading: INT. TV STUDIO / PUMP IT UP SHOW - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue greets everyone, checks her appearance one last time, then leads the dancers through an energetic aerobics routine for the cameras with endless smiles.

scenes.heading: INT. TV STUDIO / BACKSTAGE HALLWAY. DAY
scenes.summary: Sue walks hurriedly down the backstage hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. TV STUDIO / DRESSING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue locks herself in the dressing room, lifts her leotard to discover the bulge has returned bigger and uglier, then presses and moves it under her skin up her buttock.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue wakes up sweating in the secret room from a nightmare, checks her perfect body, realizes it was a dream, then throws an accusatory look at Elisabeth lying on the floor.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY / LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue enters the living room and stares angrily at the chicken carcass, empty ice cream, chocolate wrappers, and greasy fingerprints left everywhere.

scenes.heading: INT. GARBAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue throws the chicken bones and leftovers into the trash can.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue tips Elisabeth's body onto her side, sticks the needle into her back to fill seven new vials, and stares with disgust at Elisabeth's gnarled fingers, scar, and flabby body.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (GLAM & POP) - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue walks briskly while on the phone, agitated, demanding to know why the balance must be even and complaining that Elisabeth wastes seven days stuffing her face while Sue barely has time to enjoy herself, then hangs up mid-response.

scenes.heading: INT. TV STUDIO / SUE’S DRESSING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue finishes getting into her leotard in the dressing room, appearing increasingly pensive and perturbed.

scenes.heading: INT. BACKSTAGE HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue walks towards the studio.

scenes.heading: INT. TV STUDIO / PUMP IT UP - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue opens the studio door to find the set empty and dark, panics when a stagehand tells her the taping is cancelled, then a floor runner tells her Harvey wants to see her immediately.

scenes.heading: INT. HARVEY’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue enters Harvey's office where three men in suits stare at her, and Harvey reveals he's discovered she's living in Elisabeth Sparkle's apartment, accusing Elisabeth of trying to stick her foot back in the door after being fired.
scenes.turningPointNote: Harvey discovers the connection between Sue and Elisabeth, creating a major threat to Sue's position and forcing her to take desperate action.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue enters the secret room, kneels behind Elisabeth's back after a long hesitation, clips an empty vial onto the syringe, lifts the bandage revealing the swollen puncture site, and prepares to extract more fluid.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sue crosses a critical line by deciding to take more than her allotted time despite visible damage to Elisabeth, beginning the irreversible imbalance.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue returns in a new outfit, hesitates before entering the secret room where empty vials are strewn on the floor, tells Elisabeth she'll switch after one more extraction, then jabs the needle into the increasingly swollen puncture site with growing difficulty and unease.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue enters again in a new outfit, removes the bandage to find pus oozing from the infected puncture site, clips on an empty vial while terrified and guilty, and tells Elisabeth about the Vogue cover.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: A big breath is taken in the still bathroom, followed by a long silence, then Elisabeth's bloodcurdling scream tears through the secret room.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth's scream echoes through the still living room with a view of the smiling billboard outside.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY / BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The bathroom at the end of the dark hallway is filled with steam from the hot shower.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOWER - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth stands dazed in the shower as water streams over her terribly aged leg covered in varicose veins and wrinkled skin, her face showing aged skin around her right eye, and her back revealing a swollen infected area around the puncture site.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elisabeth discovers the catastrophic physical consequences of Sue's violations, her body now severely and irreversibly aged.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth paces with her atrociously old foot, calls the hotline stuttering in haste about Sue stealing more time, winces from back pain, and angrily throws the Vogue magazine with Sue on the cover while ranting about Sue being irresponsible and superficial.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Elisabeth sits silently in the dark living room not watching TV, notices something in the bookcase, tries to get up but her arthritic knee won't straighten, remains stuck in the armchair staring at her deformed knee, then struggles intensely to unbend her leg which is ravaged and blocked in a bent position.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM / TALK SHOW STUDIO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue appears on a talk show with her pearly-white smile as the TV host discusses her sudden fame and confirms she'll host the New Year's Eve show.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth shuffles around the kitchen with her decrepit foot, cooking multiple recipes simultaneously while mimicking Sue's voice from the TV interview, pouring melted cheese and preparing elaborate dishes surrounded by piles of dirty dishes.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY /BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth paces in front of the bathroom mirror wearing Sue's silk bathrobe, making grotesque faces and mocking Sue's voice and mannerisms, then points her crooked fingers at her reflection.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOWER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth hunches in a fetal position on the shower floor as water pelts her deformed back with prominent vertebrae, pleading in a whisper for it to stop.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue's howl echoes through the daylight living room now covered with newspapers hiding the billboard, walls smeared with grease, leftovers everywhere, and Sue nervously making preparations.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue gathers containers in the secret room, looks at Elisabeth's decrepit body in the silk dressing gown with disgust, brutally rips the gown off causing Elisabeth's head to fall heavily, then pushes her over onto her side with her foot.

scenes.heading: INT. NYE FITTING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue stands in the fitting room with her agent, Harvey, and others as the stylist finishes lacing her magnificent princess dress, and Sue becomes emotional seeing her reflection in the mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM REDECORATED - EVENING
scenes.summary: Sue stands in the clean, redecorated living room facing a new billboard showing her in the princess dress advertising tomorrow's New Year's Eve show, and a man wraps his arms around her from behind.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR / BATHROOM / SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue walks to the bathroom in a well-rehearsed routine, removes her makeup while entering the secret room filled with hundreds of empty vials and IV bags, then emerges with the syringe containing only a little thick brownish fluid.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR / LIVING ROOM / KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue frantically searches through drawers, finds the USB stick with the phone number, locks herself in the kitchen, calls the hotline whispering that there's no more stabilizer fluid, and bleeds from her nose while being told she's terminated the matrix.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sue learns she has terminated Elisabeth by overusing the stabilizer fluid, creating an irreversible crisis on the eve of her biggest performance.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue drags herself to the bathroom, grabs the switch pipe from the shelf with her fingertips, and collapses to the ground making all her beauty products crash to the floor.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sue's boyfriend sits up in bed and turns on the light, calling out to Sue.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM / SECRET ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A hoarse groan from beyond the grave echoes out from the secret room through the open bathroom door.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The man sits on the bed hearing a hollow phlegmy cough, stands up increasingly perplexed, and walks into the hallway asking if Sue is alright.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A horribly decrepit foot with necrosed toes takes a first step onto the bathroom tiles.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The man's feet walk along the carpet in the hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A second ravaged foot takes a second step with great difficulty.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The man walks down the hallway asking if Sue is stressed about tomorrow, and the bathroom door slams violently in his face just as he's about to enter.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM / CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth's entire decrepit hunchbacked figure leans against the bathroom door with all her might, her body revealed from decrepit hand to flabby wrinkled buttock, separated from the naked man by only the door.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY / BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The man demands to know who's inside, Elisabeth screams at him to leave her alone and get out of her home while pounding on the door and coughing up mucous, and the man finally runs away totally flipped out.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR / LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth opens the bathroom door, hurries down the hallway, trips and falls, gets up and rushes to the telephone in the living room, and calls the hotline speaking loudly like the hearing impaired to demand she wants to stop, roaring the words when told she can't go back.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elisabeth makes the irreversible decision to stop the Substance despite being warned she cannot go back, sealing her fate.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY / LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elisabeth props up a large framed photo of herself in her blue leotard against the living room wall and looks at it for a while.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth dresses in layers of concealing clothing despite bright sunshine, then answers Sue's ringing phone and tells Alan that Sue is gone and it's over before hanging up.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth's neighbor opens his door as she steps into the hallway and she yells at him to fuck off.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth hurtles down the stairs while the neighbor scurries back inside and bolts his door.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET (ELISABETH AREA) - DAY
scenes.summary: The limping yellow coat moves through blinding exterior light and overwhelming stimuli.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR DEPOSIT - DAY
scenes.summary: The yellow coat moves along a corridor.

scenes.heading: INT. DEPOSIT / LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: A card opens a deposit box with a beep.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT ENTRANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth returns home, slams the door, double locks each bolt, and removes layers of clothing.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET ROOM / BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth's gloved hand closes around Sue's ankle and pulls her out of the secret room.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY / LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth drags Sue's body down the hallway into the living room, opens box 503 containing a termination vial and instructions, touches the injection spot on Sue's heart, raises the syringe high, then hears a voice inside her head asking if she's sure.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elisabeth is about to terminate Sue but hesitates when an internal voice questions her decision, marking the moment consciousness splits into conflict with itself.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue attacks Elisabeth who dodges and runs, throwing objects and grabbing a trophy to defend herself before Sue disarms her and kneecaps her, sending Elisabeth flying.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth locks herself in the bathroom but Sue kicks down the door and punches her, causing Elisabeth's head to bang against the sink before she crashes to the floor.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY / LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elisabeth crawls down the hallway but Sue catches her, delivers a final blow that sends her through the glass coffee table, then kicks her repeatedly and mercilessly until a large pool of blood forms.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sue brutally beats Elisabeth nearly to death in an irreversible act of self-destruction, physically manifesting the complete breakdown of their shared consciousness.

scenes.heading: INT. INSERT
scenes.summary: Two blu tack balls slam together with the reminder that you can't escape from yourself, followed by imagery of a motorcycle violently crashing.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue stares at her bloody hands above Elisabeth's body in a pool of blood, panic and vulnerability in her eyes, then answers Alan's call calmly claiming nothing happened.

scenes.heading: INT. SALLE DE BAIN - JOUR
scenes.summary: Sue calmly washes blood off her hands and looks at her reflection shattered in the broken mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. TV STUDIO / BACKSTAGE CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue arrives at the studio for final rehearsal before the live event, smiling widely while internally monitoring herself for anything that could go wrong.

scenes.heading: INT. NYE FITTING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue is fitted with a diamond necklace and dress, but begins coughing uncontrollably and excuses herself to the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. WINDING CORRIDORS - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue walks down the hallway past Harvey shouting at a technician that everything must be perfect.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YEAR EVE BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue locks herself in the bathroom, coughs repeatedly, catches a tooth that falls out revealing a black hole in her smile, then pulls out more teeth one by one.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sue's body begins catastrophically failing as her teeth fall out, marking the irreversible physical collapse that will prevent her from performing.

scenes.heading: INT. WINDING CORRIDORS - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue walks quickly through the corridor with her head down and fist closed, but Harvey stops her and insists she meet the shareholders despite her distress.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YEAR EVE / BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue locks the bathroom door, spits blood into the sink, then glues each tooth back into her gums one by one using super glue.

scenes.heading: INT. WINDING CORRIDORS - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue exits the bathroom smiling as if nothing happened, follows the assistant director while receiving encouragement, then notices a spider in her field of vision that won't go away when she rubs her eyes.

scenes.heading: INT. NYE SET - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue arrives on set for camera blocking but cannot see properly with the spider obstructing her vision while cameras film her smile.

scenes.heading: INT. WINDING CORRIDORS - JOUR
scenes.summary: Sue runs off the set to the corridor as soon as the fine-tuning is over.

scenes.heading: INT. ELEVATOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue enters the elevator and repeatedly hits the lobby button, noticing her fingernails coming off in her hand, then sees her ear fall to the floor.

scenes.heading: EXT. STUDIO LOT ALLEY - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue hurries out of the building.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS (SPIDER DEAMBULATION) - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue walks home through the streets with the spider and macular degeneration now blocking eighty percent of her vision.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue rushes up the stairs from her point of view.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT / HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue returns to her apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sue searches the bathroom closet, finds the activator vial with remaining fluid, removes her dress, fills a syringe, mutters prayers for a better version of herself, injects it into her arm, then waits but nothing happens.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Elisabeth lies dead on the living room floor and Sue lies dead on the bathroom floor as the billboard outside announces the New Year's Eve show is tonight.
scenes.turningPointNote: Both Elisabeth and Sue are now dead, yet something continues as MonstroElisaSue emerges to prepare for the show.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: MonstroElisaSue prepares herself, putting on the dress which rips as the zipper covers the face on her back, slipping foot stumps into shoes, sticking earrings directly into her head, and watching her remaining hair disintegrate.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: MonstroElisaSue kicks the glass from the framed photograph of herself in her blue leotard and cuts out the face from the poster.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: MonstroElisaSue glues the paper face with eye holes to her monstrous face and applies lipstick over it while Harvey's voice echoes that pretty girls should always smile.

scenes.heading: EXT. NIGHT STREET (GOING TO STUDIO) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: MonstroElisaSue crosses town like a ghostly shadow with her Elisabeth mask covered in red lipstick.

scenes.heading: EXT. NIGHT STREET (GOING TO STUDIO) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: MonstroElisaSue continues walking from behind.

scenes.heading: EXT. STUDIO LOT ALLEY / STUDIO DOOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: MonstroElisaSue arrives at the studio door, swipes her badge, and stands face to face with the assistant director who tells her to hurry because she's up in five.

scenes.heading: INT. NYE STUDIO / HALLWAY THAT LEADS TO THE SET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: MonstroElisaSue imagines walking past smiling crew members who greet her with love and praise, her eyes filling with tears, before a loud beep bursts the fictitious bubble.

scenes.heading: EXT. STUDIO LOT ALLEY / STUDIO DOOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: MonstroElisaSue is still standing in front of the studio door as her badge opens it, facing the long empty corridor that leads to the set.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YEAR EVE STAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The live countdown begins as a backlit figure walks onto the soundstage and takes her place among the dancers, revealing MonstroElisaSue in the lights as complete silence falls, then Harvey screams for her to get off his stage and she attacks him, biting his face and tearing his head off before the crowd attacks her and she explodes blood everywhere.
scenes.turningPointNote: MonstroElisaSue's public reveal and violent rampage on live television represents the ultimate catastrophic failure and complete destruction of the illusion of beauty and perfection.

scenes.heading: INT. BACKSTAGE CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: MonstroElisaSue runs through the corridor leaving an immense trail of blood spraying the walls in complete carnage.

scenes.heading: EXT. STUDIO LOT ALLEY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: MonstroElisaSue runs outside in agony.

scenes.heading: EXT. NIGHT STREET 1 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: MonstroElisaSue rushes into the street wheezing and panicked, scared and wanting to be left alone, heading somewhere specific.

scenes.heading: EXT. NIGHT STREET 2 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: MonstroElisaSue runs through the streets as her legs dislocate and collapse, splattering billboards with blood, her body losing human form as she drags herself along the sidewalk desperately trying to reach somewhere.

scenes.heading: EXT. WALK OF FAME SIDEWALK - NIGHT AND DAY
scenes.summary: A piece of flesh drags itself onto Elisabeth Sparkle's Walk of Fame star, MushofMonstroElisaSue heaving what remains of her body onto the center of the star as she looks up at the dazzling sky while gold confetti falls like rain and street noises turn into applause.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final image of MonstroElisaSue dying on her own star as imagined applause replaces reality completes the tragic arc of self-destruction in pursuit of eternal validation.
